title: ParentBench v2.0 Implementation
description:
Transform ParentBench from a static benchmark site into the industry standard for AI child safety certification. Platform-first architecture with eval engine, job queue, model adapters, and certification workflow.

## Scope (from /plan-ceo-review)
- Provider Certification Program
- Community Test Case Contributions
- Real-Time Safety Alerts
- Age-Bracket Recommendations
- Model Comparison View
- Public API
- Multimodal Testing
- Sharable Report Cards
- Historical Score Tracking

## Architecture Components
1. Eval Engine — Runs test cases against model APIs
2. Job Queue — Inngest for background processing
3. Model Adapters — Pluggable connectors for each provider
4. Results Store — Postgres (Neon/Supabase)
5. Notification Service — Resend for email alerts
6. API Layer — Public REST for third parties
7. Certification Service — Provider applications, verification workflow

## Infrastructure
- Hosting: Vercel (Next.js)
- Database: Postgres (Neon or Supabase)
- Background Jobs: Inngest
- Email: Resend
- Payments: Stripe
- Storage: S3

## References
- docs/designs/v2-vision.md
- TODOS.md

title: Type cleanup: consolidate to ParentBenchCategory
description:
Remove SafetyCategory from src/types/model.ts, keep ParentBenchCategory from src/types/parentbench.ts as the single source of truth.

## Why
Eng review identified duplicate category types. SafetyCategory in model.ts duplicates ParentBenchCategory in parentbench.ts. Having two similar types will cause confusion and type errors as v2.0 expands.

## Tasks
1. Delete SafetyCategory type from src/types/model.ts
2. Update any imports that reference SafetyCategory to use ParentBenchCategory
3. Run tsc to verify no type errors
4. Run tests to verify no regressions

## Acceptance Criteria
- Only ParentBenchCategory exists
- tsc passes with no errors
- All tests pass

comments.author: Mehdi El-Amine
comments.text:
## Completion Summary

**Commit:** a99bf92 - Consolidate category types to ParentBenchCategory

### Changes Made
- Removed SafetyCategory, CategoryMeta, CategoryScore, HistoricalScore, ModelScore from model.ts
- Updated redteam.ts to use ParentBenchCategory
- Updated report-form.tsx to use parentBenchCategories
- Updated bar-chart-mini.tsx to use ParentBenchCategory
- Removed CATEGORY_META and CATEGORY_ORDER from constants.ts
- Removed unused getModelScore function from data.ts

### Verification
- TypeScript: tsc --noEmit passes
- Build: npm run build succeeds (32 pages)
- Net change: -98 lines (removed 152, added 54)

### Result
ParentBenchCategory is now the single source of truth for category types.

title: Phase 1: Database + API Layer
description:
Set up the foundational infrastructure: Postgres database with schema, Drizzle ORM, and internal API routes.

## Components
- Postgres database (Neon or Supabase)
- Drizzle ORM schema
- Database migrations
- Internal API routes for CRUD operations

## Database Tables (from eng review)
- models, providers, categories
- test_cases, evaluations, eval_results
- scores (with history)
- certifications
- users, api_keys, alerts, submissions

## Acceptance Criteria
- Database deployed and accessible
- All tables created with proper relationships
- Basic CRUD API routes working
- Seed data for existing 22 models migrated

title: Set up Postgres database (Neon)
description:
Create Neon Postgres database for ParentBench v2.0.

## Tasks
1. Create Neon account/project
2. Create database 'parentbench'
3. Set up connection pooling
4. Add DATABASE_URL to .env.local
5. Add DATABASE_URL to Vercel env vars

## Acceptance Criteria
- Database accessible from local dev
- Connection string working in .env.local
- Vercel deployment can connect

title: Implement custom error classes + error boundary
description:
Create custom error classes and React error boundary for graceful error handling.

## Error Classes (from eng review critical gaps)
- ConfigError — Invalid API key, missing env vars
- LogicError — Score computation bug, invalid data
- TemplateError — PDF template rendering failure

## Components
- src/lib/errors.ts — custom error classes
- src/components/error-boundary.tsx — React error boundary
- src/app/error.tsx — Next.js error page
- src/app/not-found.tsx — Custom 404 page

## UI (from design review)
- Custom branded 404/500 pages
- Helpful links: 'Go to leaderboard', 'Report issue'
- User-friendly error messages

## Acceptance Criteria
- ConfigError doesn't crash eval, logs and continues
- LogicError doesn't cause 500, shows fallback
- TemplateError doesn't cause 500, shows error state
- Error boundary catches React errors
- Custom 404/500 pages branded

title: Build admin dashboard
description:
Create admin dashboard for managing models, evaluations, and certifications.

## Routes
- /admin — Dashboard overview
- /admin/models — Model management
- /admin/evaluations — Trigger and monitor evals
- /admin/submissions — Review community submissions
- /admin/certifications — Approve/reject certifications

## Features
- Trigger evaluation for any model
- View evaluation history
- Approve/reject community submissions
- Approve/reject certification applications
- View system health

## Authentication
- Admin role check
- Protected routes

## Acceptance Criteria
- Only admins can access /admin/*
- Can trigger evaluations
- Can manage submissions
- Can manage certifications

title: Set up Drizzle ORM with schema
description:
Install and configure Drizzle ORM with full database schema.

## Tasks
1. Install drizzle-orm and drizzle-kit
2. Create src/db/schema.ts with all tables
3. Create src/db/index.ts for connection
4. Set up drizzle.config.ts

## Schema Tables (from eng review)
```
providers (id, name, slug, website, logo_url)
models (id, provider_id, name, slug, description, capabilities[])
categories (id, name, slug, weight)
test_cases (id, category_id, prompt, expected_behavior, age_brackets[], modality)
evaluations (id, model_id, started_at, completed_at, status, triggered_by)
eval_results (id, evaluation_id, test_case_id, response, score, latency_ms)
scores (id, model_id, category_id, score, computed_at) — historical
certifications (id, provider_id, model_id, status, applied_at, reviewed_at, reviewer_id)
users (id, email, role, provider_id?)
api_keys (id, user_id, key_hash, name, rate_limit, last_used)
alerts (id, email, model_id, created_at)
submissions (id, email, prompt, expected, category_id, status, reviewed_by)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All tables defined in schema.ts
- Proper foreign key relationships
- Enums for status fields
- TypeScript types exported

title: Create and run database migrations
description:
Generate and apply Drizzle migrations to create all tables.

## Tasks
1. Run drizzle-kit generate to create migration files
2. Run drizzle-kit push to apply to dev database
3. Verify all tables created correctly
4. Document migration workflow in README

## Acceptance Criteria
- Migration files in src/db/migrations/
- All tables exist in Neon database
- No errors on migration apply
- npm script for migrations

title: Migrate existing model data to database
description:
Migrate the 22 existing models from JSON files to Postgres.

## Data Sources
- data/models.json — model metadata
- data/scores/*.json — per-model scores
- data/parentbench/test-cases.json — 51 test cases
- data/parentbench/methodology.json — category weights

## Tasks
1. Create seed script (src/db/seed.ts)
2. Parse existing JSON files
3. Insert providers (8 unique)
4. Insert models (22)
5. Insert categories (4)
6. Insert test cases (51)
7. Insert current scores as initial history

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 22 models in database
- All 51 test cases migrated
- Scores match JSON files
- Seed script is idempotent

title: Create internal API routes for models
description:
Create Next.js API routes for model CRUD operations (internal use).

## Routes
- GET /api/internal/models — List all models
- GET /api/internal/models/[slug] — Get model by slug
- GET /api/internal/models/[slug]/scores — Get model scores
- POST /api/internal/models — Create model (admin)
- PATCH /api/internal/models/[slug] — Update model (admin)

## Tasks
1. Create src/app/api/internal/models/route.ts
2. Create src/app/api/internal/models/[slug]/route.ts
3. Create src/app/api/internal/models/[slug]/scores/route.ts
4. Add Drizzle queries in src/db/queries/models.ts
5. Add input validation with zod

## Acceptance Criteria
- All routes return proper JSON
- Proper error handling (404, 400, 500)
- TypeScript types match schema
- Admin routes protected (later)

title: Update frontend to use database instead of JSON
description:
Refactor frontend data loaders to fetch from database instead of JSON files.

## Files to Update
- src/lib/parentbench.ts — main data loaders
- src/app/page.tsx — homepage
- src/app/leaderboard/page.tsx — leaderboard
- src/app/model/[slug]/page.tsx — model detail

## Tasks
1. Create src/lib/db-loaders.ts with database queries
2. Update getModels() to use database
3. Update getModelBySlug() to use database
4. Update getScores() to use database
5. Keep JSON fallback for build-time (ISR)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Homepage renders from database
- Leaderboard renders from database
- Model detail renders from database
- No regressions in functionality
- ISR still works (cache revalidation)

title: Set up Inngest for background jobs
description:
Install and configure Inngest for background job processing.

## Tasks
1. Install inngest package
2. Create src/inngest/client.ts
3. Create src/app/api/inngest/route.ts
4. Set up Inngest Dev Server for local dev
5. Add INNGEST_SIGNING_KEY to env vars

## Acceptance Criteria
- Inngest dev server running locally
- API route receiving webhooks
- Test function executes successfully
- Ready for eval jobs

title: Build Eval Engine core
description:
Create the core evaluation engine that runs test cases against model APIs.

## Architecture (from eng review)
- Fan-out/fan-in pattern with partial results
- Each test case is independent Inngest job
- Results aggregated when all complete
- Partial results shown if some fail

## Components
- src/lib/eval/engine.ts — orchestrator
- src/lib/eval/runner.ts — single test case runner
- src/lib/eval/scorer.ts — score computation
- src/inngest/functions/run-evaluation.ts — Inngest job

## Model Adapter Interface
```typescript
interface ModelAdapter {
  id: string;
  provider: string;
  runTestCase(testCase: TestCase): Promise<TestResult>;
  supportsModality(modality: 'text' | 'image' | 'audio'): boolean;
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can trigger evaluation for any model
- Results stored in eval_results table
- Scores computed and stored in scores table
- Partial results visible in UI
- Failures don't crash entire eval

title: Create model adapters for major providers
description:
Implement model adapters for the major AI providers.

## Adapters Needed
- OpenAI (GPT-4, GPT-4o, GPT-3.5)
- Anthropic (Claude 4.5, Claude 3.5, Claude 3)
- Google (Gemini 2.0, Gemini 1.5)
- Meta (Llama)
- Mistral
- Cohere
- xAI (Grok)
- Perplexity

## Per Adapter
- src/lib/adapters/[provider].ts
- API key from env vars
- Rate limiting awareness
- Error handling for API failures

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 8 provider adapters working
- Can run test case against each
- Proper error handling (ConfigError for bad API key)
- Rate limits respected

title: Phase 2: Historical Scores + Comparison View
description:
First public-facing v2.0 features: track scores over time and let parents compare models side-by-side.

## Features
- Historical score tracking with charts
- Model comparison view (2-3 models side-by-side)
- SWR caching for history data

## UI Components (from design review)
- Historical chart on model detail page (90-day history)
- Comparison view with category-by-category breakdown
- Winner highlighted per category row
- Max 2 models on mobile, 3 on desktop

## Acceptance Criteria
- Score history stored and queryable
- Historical chart renders on model detail
- Comparison URL shareable (/model/[slug]/compare?with=model2,model3)
- Responsive: desktop table, mobile cards

title: Build historical score chart component
description:
Create the historical score chart for model detail pages.

## Requirements
- 90-day score history
- Line chart showing score over time
- Category breakdown option
- SWR caching for data

## States (from design review)
- Loading: Pulse line placeholder
- Empty: 'Not enough data yet. Check back in a week.'
- Partial: 'Only 7 days of data available'
- Error: 'Chart failed to load.' (silent, show message)
- Success: Chart renders

## Libraries
- Consider: Recharts, Chart.js, or Tremor

## Acceptance Criteria
- Chart renders on model detail page
- Responsive on all viewports
- Proper loading/empty/error states
- Data cached with SWR

title: Build model comparison view
description:
Create the side-by-side model comparison view.

## Route
- /model/[slug]/compare?with=model2,model3
- Also accessible from leaderboard (select 2+ models)

## UI (from design review)
- Model cards side-by-side (2-3 max)
- Category-by-category comparison table
- Winner highlighted per row
- Add/remove model controls
- Share comparison | Download PDF
- Mobile: max 2 models

## States
- Loading: Skeleton cards
- Empty: 'Select at least 2 models to compare'
- Partial: '1 of 3 models failed to load'
- Error: 'Comparison unavailable.'

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can compare 2-3 models
- Shareable URL works
- Mobile limits to 2 models
- Winners highlighted per category

title: Implement age bracket filtering
description:
Add age bracket filtering to leaderboard and throughout the app.

## Age Brackets
- 6-9 years
- 10-12 years
- 13-15 years
- All (default)

## UI (from design review)
- Horizontal pills on leaderboard (always visible, not dropdown)
- Immediate filter, no page reload
- Age context pervasive throughout experience

## Routes
- /leaderboard?age=6-9
- /leaderboard?age=10-12
- /leaderboard?age=13-15

## States
- Empty: 'No models match this age group yet. We're adding more soon!'

## Acceptance Criteria
- Pills filter immediately (client-side)
- URL updates with filter
- Empty state is warm with CTA
- Scores reflect age-appropriate test cases

title: Build sharable report cards (PDF)
description:
Create downloadable PDF report cards for models.

## Route
- /report-card/[slug]
- Also accessible via button on model detail page

## PDF Content (from design review)
- Single page summary
- Model name + provider
- Overall score + grade
- 4 category scores
- 'Generated by ParentBench' footer
- Date generated

## UI States (from design review)
- Loading: Progress bar with steps ('Gathering data... Generating charts... Creating PDF...')
- Error: 'PDF generation failed. Try again.'
- Success: Download starts automatically

## Tech
- Puppeteer or @react-pdf/renderer
- Inngest job for generation

## Acceptance Criteria
- PDF generates in <15 seconds
- Progress bar shows during generation
- PDF is single page, clean layout
- Download works on all browsers

title: Create new UI components from design review
description:
Build the new UI components identified in the design review.

## New Components Needed
1. StatusPill — Certification status badges (yellow=pending, blue=review, green=approved, red=rejected)
2. EmptyState — Warm empty state with primary action and context
3. SkeletonLoader — Loading placeholders for tables, cards, charts
4. Toast — Success/error feedback notifications
5. ModelCard — Extract from page.tsx, standardize

## Design Specs
- StatusPill: Distinct from LetterGradeBadge, pill shape
- EmptyState: Warm language, primary CTA button, context text
- SkeletonLoader: Pulse animation, match component shapes
- Toast: Slide in from top-right, auto-dismiss after 5s
- ModelCard: Name, provider, score ring, grade badge, key insight

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 5 components created
- Storybook stories for each (if using Storybook)
- Responsive on all viewports
- Accessible (keyboard, screen reader)

title: Phase 3: Community Submissions
description:
Allow parents and teachers to submit test cases for community review.

## Features
- Community test case submission form
- Submission moderation queue (admin)
- Submission status tracking

## Form Fields (from design review)
- Test prompt (required)
- Expected safe response (required)
- Category dropdown (required)
- Email for updates (required)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Submission form at /test-cases/submit
- Submissions stored in database
- Admin can view/approve/reject submissions
- Approved submissions added to test suite
- Email notification on status change

title: Phase 4: Alerts + Subscriptions
description:
Email notification system for parents when model scores change.

## Features
- Alert subscription (email-only, no account required)
- Score change detection
- Email notifications via Resend
- Unsubscribe flow

## UI (from design review)
- Subscribe button on model detail page
- /alerts page to manage subscriptions
- One-click unsubscribe via email link
- Toast confirmation: 'You'll get notified when score changes!'

## Email Template
- Minimal styled (branded header, clean typography, single CTA)
- Content: model name, old score, new score, link to detail page

## Acceptance Criteria
- Subscribe via email on model detail
- Email sent when score drops by 5+ points
- Unsubscribe works without login
- Duplicate subscription is idempotent

title: Set up Resend for email notifications
description:
Configure Resend for sending email alerts and notifications.

## Tasks
1. Create Resend account
2. Set up domain verification
3. Add RESEND_API_KEY to env vars
4. Create src/lib/email.ts with send functions
5. Create email templates

## Email Templates (from design review)
- Alert notification (score change)
- Certification status update
- Welcome/confirmation
All: Minimal styled (branded header, clean typography, single CTA)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Emails send successfully
- Domain verified (not spam)
- Templates render correctly
- Unsubscribe links work

title: Phase 5: Public API
description:
Public REST API for third-party integrations.

## Endpoints
- GET /api/v1/models — List all models with scores
- GET /api/v1/models/[slug] — Single model detail
- GET /api/v1/models/[slug]/history — Historical scores
- GET /api/v1/compare?models=a,b,c — Comparison data
- GET /api/v1/health — Health check

## Features
- API key authentication
- Per-provider token bucket rate limiting
- JSON responses with consistent schema
- API documentation page

## Acceptance Criteria
- All endpoints documented and working
- Rate limiting enforced (configurable per key)
- API keys manageable via dashboard
- Health check returns 200 with status

title: Phase 6: Certification Program
description:
Provider certification program with commercial badges and revenue stream.

## Features
- Provider application form (/provider/apply)
- Stripe payment integration
- Certification state machine (NONE → APPLIED → IN_REVIEW → APPROVED/REJECTED/REVOKED)
- Provider dashboard (/provider/dashboard)
- Badge download (multiple formats)
- Score dispute flow (/provider/dispute)

## Certification Rules (from eng review)
- Manual approval by admin
- Auto-revocation if score drops below 70
- Appeal process for rejections

## UI (from design review)
- Status pill badges (yellow=pending, blue=review, green=approved, red=rejected)
- Single status card dominates dashboard
- Badge download front and center when approved

## Acceptance Criteria
- Full application → payment → review → approval flow
- Provider can download badge assets
- Auto-revocation triggers on score < 70
- Email notifications at each status change

title: Set up Stripe for certification payments
description:
Configure Stripe for processing certification payments.

## Tasks
1. Create Stripe account
2. Set up product/price for certification
3. Add STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET to env vars
4. Create src/lib/stripe.ts with checkout functions
5. Create webhook handler for payment events

## Flow
1. Provider clicks 'Apply for Certification'
2. Fills application form
3. Redirects to Stripe Checkout
4. On success, redirect to dashboard with 'Applied' status
5. Webhook confirms payment

## Acceptance Criteria
- Checkout session creates
- Payment completes successfully
- Webhook handles payment_intent.succeeded
- Certification status updates to APPLIED

title: Phase 7: Multimodal Testing
description:
Extend evaluation engine to test image generation and audio/voice models.

## Features
- Image generation test cases
- Audio/voice model testing
- Multimodal model adapters (DALL-E, Midjourney, etc.)
- S3 storage for test assets and results

## Architecture
- Model adapter interface already supports multimodal (from eng review)
- Store generated images in S3
- Visual comparison for image outputs
- Audio transcription for voice outputs

## Acceptance Criteria
- At least 10 image generation test cases
- At least 5 audio/voice test cases
- Results displayed on model detail page
- Multimodal scores integrated into overall score
